1→# Phase 28: Observability - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Domain:** Kubernetes observability — OpenTelemetry Collector + Aspire Dashboard standalone
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 28 adds observability to the kind cluster by deploying two new services: an OpenTelemetry (OTEL) Collector and the .NET Aspire Dashboard in standalone mode. The existing .NET services (ApiService, Gateway) already have full OpenTelemetry instrumentation via `MicroCommerce.ServiceDefaults` — they configure logging, tracing (including MassTransit spans), and metrics, and conditionally enable the OTLP exporter when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set. Today in the K8s manifests, that env var is absent, so telemetry is generated but discarded. This phase wires it up.
    10→
    11→The architecture is: **Apps -> OTEL Collector -> Aspire Dashboard**. The Collector provides a stable internal OTLP endpoint, adds batch processing and memory limiting, and forwards to the Dashboard. The Dashboard runs as an in-memory dev tool (data clears on pod restart) with anonymous access enabled for the dev cluster. Both services get Kustomize base manifests, ArgoCD Application YAMLs, and a NodePort for Dashboard UI access.
    12→
    13→**Primary recommendation:** Deploy OTEL Collector as a Deployment (not DaemonSet) with a minimal config (OTLP receiver, memory_limiter, batch processor, OTLP exporter to Aspire Dashboard). Deploy Aspire Dashboard with `DOTNET_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS=true`. Set `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` and `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` env vars on ApiService and Gateway deployments. Skip `k8sattributes` processor to avoid RBAC complexity — unnecessary for a dev showcase cluster.
    14→
    15→<phase_requirements>
    16→## Phase Requirements
    17→
    18→| ID | Description | Research Support |
    19→|----|-------------|-----------------|
    20→| OBSV-01 | OTEL Collector receives OTLP from all app services and forwards to Aspire Dashboard | OTEL Collector deployed as K8s Deployment with OTLP gRPC receiver (port 4317), batch processor, memory_limiter, and OTLP exporter to aspire-dashboard:18889. ApiService and Gateway set `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:4317`. |
    21→| OBSV-02 | Aspire Dashboard runs as a standalone container in K8s accessible for dev monitoring | `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire-dashboard:9.3` deployed as K8s Deployment. UI on port 18888, OTLP ingestion on port 18889. NodePort 30888 -> host 38888 for local browser access. Anonymous auth via `DOTNET_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS=true`. |
    22→</phase_requirements>
    23→
    24→## Standard Stack
    25→
    26→### Core
    27→
    28→| Component | Image/Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    29→|-----------|---------------|---------|--------------|
    30→| OTEL Collector (core) | `otel/opentelemetry-collector:0.146.0` | Receive, process, and export telemetry | Core distribution is sufficient — we only need OTLP receiver, batch processor, memory_limiter, and OTLP exporter. No need for contrib distribution (no k8sattributes, no Prometheus). Pinned version avoids `latest` tag drift. |
    31→| Aspire Dashboard (standalone) | `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire-dashboard:9.3` | Visualize traces, metrics, logs | Official Microsoft image. Use `9.3` tag for stability (latest stable standalone release). The project uses Aspire 13.1.0 SDK but the standalone dashboard image follows its own versioning. `latest` tag also works. |
    32→
    33→### Supporting
    34→
    35→| Component | Purpose | When to Use |
    36→|-----------|---------|-------------|
    37→| `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` env var | Enables OTLP export in .NET apps | Set on ApiService and Gateway K8s Deployments to point to `http://otel-collector:4317` |
    38→| `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` env var | Identifies service in dashboard traces | Set to `apiservice` and `gateway` respectively — matches Aspire AppHost naming |
    39→| `DOTNET_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS` | Disables Dashboard login | Set to `true` for dev cluster — no auth needed in kind |
    40→
    41→### Alternatives Considered
    42→
    43→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
    44→|------------|-----------|----------|
    45→| OTEL Collector core | OTEL Collector contrib (`otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib`) | Contrib adds k8sattributes, Prometheus exporter, etc. — unnecessary for this phase. Core is smaller image, fewer dependencies. |
    46→| OTEL Collector Deployment | OTEL Collector DaemonSet | DaemonSet runs on every node — overkill for single-node kind cluster. Deployment with 1 replica is simpler. |
    47→| Skip OTEL Collector (apps -> Dashboard directly) | No Collector | Works technically, but violates OBSV-01 requirement and loses batch processing, memory limiting, and future extensibility. |
    48→| k8sattributes processor | No k8s metadata enrichment | k8sattributes requires RBAC (ServiceAccount, ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBinding) and contrib image. Not worth complexity for dev showcase where service names already identify workloads. |
    49→| Aspire Dashboard `9.3` tag | `latest` tag | `latest` may introduce breaking changes. Pinned tag is reproducible. However, `latest` is acceptable for dev. |
    50→
    51→## Architecture Patterns
    52→
    53→### Telemetry Flow
    54→
    55→```
    56→ApiService (port 8080)
    57→  OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:4317
    58→  OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=apiservice
    59→  |
    60→  |--- OTLP gRPC --->  OTEL Collector (port 4317)
    61→                          |
    62→Gateway (port 8080)       |--- memory_limiter
    63→  OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector:4317
    64→  OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=gateway    |--- batch processor
    65→  |                            |
    66→  |--- OTLP gRPC --->         |--- OTLP exporter ---> Aspire Dashboard (port 18889)
    67→                                                          |
    68→                                                     Dashboard UI (port 18888)
    69→                                                          |
    70→                                                     NodePort 30888 -> host 38888
    71→                                                          |
    72→                                                     Browser: http://localhost:38888
    73→```
    74→
    75→### Kustomize Structure
    76→
    77→```
    78→infra/k8s/base/
    79→├── otel-collector/
    80→│   ├── kustomization.yaml        # resources: configmap.yaml, deployment.yaml, service.yaml
    81→│   ├── configmap.yaml            # OTEL Collector config (receivers, processors, exporters, pipelines)
    82→│   ├── deployment.yaml           # Deployment with 1 replica, configmap volume mount
    83→│   └── service.yaml              # ClusterIP service on port 4317 (gRPC) + port 4318 (HTTP)
    84→├── aspire-dashboard/
    85→│   ├── kustomization.yaml        # resources: deployment.yaml, service.yaml
    86→│   ├── deployment.yaml           # Deployment with env vars for anonymous auth
    87→│   └── service.yaml              # ClusterIP + NodePort (30888 for UI access)
    88→└── kustomization.yaml            # Add otel-collector/ and aspire-dashboard/ to resources list
    89→```
    90→
    91→### ArgoCD Integration
    92→
    93→```
    94→infra/k8s/argocd/apps/
    95→├── otel-collector.yaml           # ArgoCD Application pointing to base/otel-collector
    96→└── aspire-dashboard.yaml         # ArgoCD Application pointing to base/aspire-dashboard
    97→```
    98→
    99→Both use the same pattern as existing apps (source: `infra/k8s/base/{service}`, destination: `micro-commerce` namespace, automated sync with selfHeal and prune).
   100→
   101→Note: These services do NOT need overlay directories since they use fixed public images (not built by CI). The base manifests are sufficient.
   102→
   103→### Pattern 1: OTEL Collector ConfigMap
   104→
   105→**What:** OTEL Collector config in a Kubernetes ConfigMap, mounted as a file.
   106→**When to use:** Always — Collector config is YAML, not container args.
   107→**Example:**
   108→
   109→```yaml
   110→# Source: OpenTelemetry Collector official docs
   111→apiVersion: v1
   112→kind: ConfigMap
   113→metadata:
   114→  name: otel-collector-config
   115→data:
   116→  otel-collector-config.yaml: |
   117→    receivers:
   118→      otlp:
   119→        protocols:
   120→          grpc:
   121→            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
   122→          http:
   123→            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
   124→
   125→    processors:
   126→      memory_limiter:
   127→        check_interval: 5s
   128→        limit_percentage: 80
   129→        spike_limit_percentage: 25
   130→      batch:
   131→        timeout: 5s
   132→        send_batch_size: 1024
   133→
   134→    exporters:
   135→      otlp/aspire:
   136→        endpoint: aspire-dashboard:18889
   137→        tls:
   138→          insecure: true
   139→
   140→    service:
   141→      pipelines:
   142→        traces:
   143→          receivers: [otlp]
   144→          processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
   145→          exporters: [otlp/aspire]
   146→        metrics:
   147→          receivers: [otlp]
   148→          processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
   149→          exporters: [otlp/aspire]
   150→        logs:
   151→          receivers: [otlp]
   152→          processors: [memory_limiter, batch]
   153→          exporters: [otlp/aspire]
   154→```
   155→
   156→### Pattern 2: OTEL Collector Deployment
   157→
   158→**What:** K8s Deployment mounting the ConfigMap.
   159→**Example:**
   160→
   161→```yaml
   162→apiVersion: apps/v1
   163→kind: Deployment
   164→metadata:
   165→  name: otel-collector
   166→  labels:
   167→    app: otel-collector
   168→spec:
   169→  replicas: 1
   170→  selector:
   171→    matchLabels:
   172→      app: otel-collector
   173→  template:
   174→    metadata:
   175→      labels:
   176→        app: otel-collector
   177→    spec:
   178→      containers:
   179→      - name: otel-collector
   180→        image: otel/opentelemetry-collector:0.146.0
   181→        args: ["--config=/etc/otelcol/otel-collector-config.yaml"]
   182→        ports:
   183→        - containerPort: 4317
   184→          name: otlp-grpc
   185→        - containerPort: 4318
   186→          name: otlp-http
   187→        - containerPort: 13133
   188→          name: health
   189→        volumeMounts:
   190→        - name: config
   191→          mountPath: /etc/otelcol
   192→        livenessProbe:
   193→          httpGet:
   194→            path: /
   195→            port: 13133
   196→          periodSeconds: 15
   197→        readinessProbe:
   198→          httpGet:
   199→            path: /
   200→            port: 13133
   201→          periodSeconds: 10
   202→        resources:
   203→          requests:
   204→            memory: 64Mi
   205→            cpu: 50m
   206→          limits:
   207→            memory: 256Mi
   208→            cpu: 200m
   209→      volumes:
   210→      - name: config
   211→        configMap:
   212→          name: otel-collector-config
   213→```
   214→
   215→### Pattern 3: Aspire Dashboard Standalone Deployment
   216→
   217→**What:** Dashboard as a K8s Deployment with anonymous auth.
   218→**Example:**
   219→
   220→```yaml
   221→apiVersion: apps/v1
   222→kind: Deployment
   223→metadata:
   224→  name: aspire-dashboard
   225→  labels:
   226→    app: aspire-dashboard
   227→spec:
   228→  replicas: 1
   229→  selector:
   230→    matchLabels:
   231→      app: aspire-dashboard
   232→  template:
   233→    metadata:
   234→      labels:
   235→        app: aspire-dashboard
   236→    spec:
   237→      containers:
   238→      - name: aspire-dashboard
   239→        image: mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire-dashboard:9.3
   240→        ports:
   241→        - containerPort: 18888
   242→          name: ui
   243→        - containerPort: 18889
   244→          name: otlp-grpc
   245→        env:
   246→        - name: DOTNET_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS
   247→          value: "true"
   248→        - name: DASHBOARD__OTLP__AUTHMODE
   249→          value: Unsecured
   250→        livenessProbe:
   251→          httpGet:
   252→            path: /
   253→            port: 18888
   254→          initialDelaySeconds: 5
   255→          periodSeconds: 15
   256→        readinessProbe:
   257→          httpGet:
   258→            path: /
   259→            port: 18888
   260→          periodSeconds: 10
   261→        resources:
   262→          requests:
   263→            memory: 128Mi
   264→            cpu: 100m
   265→          limits:
   266→            memory: 512Mi
   267→            cpu: 250m
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Pattern 4: App Deployment Env Var Patch
   271→
   272→**What:** Add OTEL env vars to existing ApiService and Gateway deployments.
   273→**Example (ApiService additions):**
   274→
   275→```yaml
   276→# Added to infra/k8s/base/apiservice/deployment.yaml env section
   277→- name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
   278→  value: "http://otel-collector:4317"
   279→- name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
   280→  value: "apiservice"
   281→```
   282→
   283→The ServiceDefaults code already checks `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` and enables the OTLP exporter:
   284→```csharp
   285→// From src/MicroCommerce.ServiceDefaults/Extensions.cs
   286→var useOtlpExporter = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(builder.Configuration["OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT"]);
   287→if (useOtlpExporter)
   288→{
   289→    builder.Services.AddOpenTelemetry().UseOtlpExporter();
   290→}
   291→```
   292→
   293→No code changes needed — only K8s manifest changes.
   294→
   295→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   296→
   297→- **Apps exporting directly to Aspire Dashboard (skipping Collector):** Violates OBSV-01 requirement. Loses batch processing, memory limiting, and the ability to add backends later without changing app config.
   298→- **Using `latest` image tag for OTEL Collector:** Tag drift causes silent breakage. Always pin a specific version.
   299→- **DaemonSet for OTEL Collector on single-node kind:** Wasteful. Deployment with 1 replica is correct for this topology.
   300→- **Adding k8sattributes without RBAC:** Collector crashes on startup with permission denied errors. Either add full RBAC chain or skip k8sattributes entirely.
   301→- **Forgetting `tls: insecure: true` on OTLP exporter to Dashboard:** Dashboard runs HTTP internally. Without this, Collector fails to connect with TLS handshake error.
   302→
   303→## Don't Hand-Roll
   304→
   305→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   306→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   307→| Telemetry collection | Custom sidecar or log scraper | OTEL Collector | Standard, well-tested, community-maintained |
   308→| Telemetry visualization | Custom dashboard | Aspire Dashboard standalone | Microsoft-maintained, OTLP-native, zero config |
   309→| OTLP export from .NET | Custom exporter middleware | `OpenTelemetry.Exporter.OpenTelemetryProtocol` via ServiceDefaults | Already wired up — just set env var |
   310→| Memory protection for Collector | Hope for the best | `memory_limiter` processor | Prevents OOM kills that take down entire observability pipeline |
   311→
   312→**Key insight:** The .NET apps are already fully instrumented. This phase is pure K8s manifest work — no application code changes needed.
   313→
   314→## Common Pitfalls
   315→
   316→### Pitfall 1: Missing `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` Env Var
   317→
   318→**What goes wrong:** All traces appear as "unknown_service" in the Dashboard, making it impossible to distinguish ApiService from Gateway.
   319→**Why it happens:** .NET OTEL SDK defaults `service.name` to "unknown_service:processname" when `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` is not set. In Aspire mode, the AppHost sets this automatically, but in K8s there is no AppHost.
   320→**How to avoid:** Set `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` env var on every instrumented Deployment.
   321→**Warning signs:** Traces in Dashboard all show the same generic service name.
   322→
   323→### Pitfall 2: Skipping `memory_limiter` Processor
   324→
   325→**What goes wrong:** Under burst traffic, OTEL Collector consumes unbounded memory and gets OOM-killed by Kubernetes.
   326→**Why it happens:** Without `memory_limiter`, the Collector buffers all incoming telemetry in memory. The `batch` processor alone does not limit memory.
   327→**How to avoid:** Always include `memory_limiter` as the FIRST processor in every pipeline. Use `limit_percentage: 80` and `spike_limit_percentage: 25` for containerized deployments.
   328→**Warning signs:** OOM kills on the otel-collector pod, gaps in telemetry data.
   329→
   330→### Pitfall 3: Wrong Aspire Dashboard OTLP Port
   331→
   332→**What goes wrong:** OTEL Collector cannot connect to Dashboard. Traces never appear.
   333→**Why it happens:** Dashboard UI is port 18888, but OTLP ingestion is port **18889** (gRPC) or 18890 (HTTP). Easy to confuse the two.
   334→**How to avoid:** OTEL Collector exporter must target port 18889, not 18888. The Service should expose 18889 for OTLP.
   335→**Warning signs:** Connection refused errors in Collector logs.
   336→
   337→### Pitfall 4: kind Port Mapping Not Updated
   338→
   339→**What goes wrong:** Dashboard is running in cluster but unreachable from host browser.
   340→**Why it happens:** The kind-config.yaml must map a containerPort for the Dashboard's NodePort. If the kind cluster was already created without this mapping, it must be recreated.
   341→**How to avoid:** Add the Aspire Dashboard NodePort mapping to kind-config.yaml. Document that existing clusters must be recreated (`kind delete cluster && bootstrap.sh`).
   342→**Warning signs:** `curl localhost:38888` refuses connection even though `kubectl port-forward` works.
   343→
   344→### Pitfall 5: OTEL Collector Using `tls: insecure: false` (default)
   345→
   346→**What goes wrong:** Collector cannot export to Dashboard because it tries TLS against an HTTP endpoint.
   347→**Why it happens:** The OTLP exporter defaults to TLS. Dashboard's internal OTLP endpoint is plain HTTP.
   348→**How to avoid:** Always set `tls: insecure: true` on the `otlp/aspire` exporter.
   349→**Warning signs:** "TLS handshake" or "certificate" errors in Collector logs.
   350→
   351→### Pitfall 6: ArgoCD Application Missing for New Services
   352→
   353→**What goes wrong:** OTEL Collector and Dashboard are in base manifests but ArgoCD never deploys them.
   354→**Why it happens:** ArgoCD app-of-apps pattern requires an Application YAML per service in `infra/k8s/argocd/apps/`.
   355→**How to avoid:** Create `otel-collector.yaml` and `aspire-dashboard.yaml` in the ArgoCD apps directory, following the existing pattern.
   356→**Warning signs:** `kubectl get applications -n argocd` does not show the new services.
   357→
   358→## Code Examples
   359→
   360→### OTEL Collector Health Check
   361→
   362→The Collector exposes health on port 13133 by default:
   363→
   364→```yaml
   365→# Source: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/tree/main/extension/healthcheckextension
   366→extensions:
   367→  health_check:
   368→    endpoint: 0.0.0.0:13133
   369→
   370→service:
   371→  extensions: [health_check]
   372→  pipelines:
   373→    # ...
   374→```
   375→
   376→Note: The health_check extension is included by default in the core distribution and is enabled automatically. You only need to explicitly configure it if you want to change the port.
   377→
   378→### Complete OTEL Collector Service YAML
   379→
   380→```yaml
   381→apiVersion: v1
   382→kind: Service
   383→metadata:
   384→  name: otel-collector
   385→spec:
   386→  selector:
   387→    app: otel-collector
   388→  ports:
   389→  - port: 4317
   390→    targetPort: 4317
   391→    name: otlp-grpc
   392→  - port: 4318
   393→    targetPort: 4318
   394→    name: otlp-http
   395→```
   396→
   397→ClusterIP only — no NodePort needed. Only accessed by ApiService/Gateway within the cluster.
   398→
   399→### Complete Aspire Dashboard Service YAML
   400→
   401→```yaml
   402→apiVersion: v1
   403→kind: Service
   404→metadata:
   405→  name: aspire-dashboard
   406→spec:
   407→  selector:
   408→    app: aspire-dashboard
   409→  ports:
   410→  - port: 18888
   411→    targetPort: 18888
   412→    name: ui
   413→  - port: 18889
   414→    targetPort: 18889
   415→    name: otlp-grpc
   416→---
   417→apiVersion: v1
   418→kind: Service
   419→metadata:
   420→  name: aspire-dashboard-nodeport
   421→spec:
   422→  type: NodePort
   423→  selector:
   424→    app: aspire-dashboard
   425→  ports:
   426→  - port: 18888
   427→    targetPort: 18888
   428→    nodePort: 30888
   429→    name: ui
   430→```
   431→
   432→Separate ClusterIP (for internal OTLP) and NodePort (for host browser access).
   433→
   434→### ArgoCD Application YAML (Example for otel-collector)
   435→
   436→```yaml
   437→# Source: follows existing pattern from infra/k8s/argocd/apps/apiservice.yaml
   438→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   439→kind: Application
   440→metadata:
   441→  name: otel-collector
   442→  namespace: argocd
   443→  finalizers:
   444→  - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
   445→spec:
   446→  project: default
   447→  source:
   448→    repoURL: https://github.com/baotoq/micro-commerce.git
   449→    targetRevision: master
   450→    path: infra/k8s/base/otel-collector
   451→  destination:
   452→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   453→    namespace: micro-commerce
   454→  syncPolicy:
   455→    automated:
   456→      selfHeal: true
   457→      prune: true
   458→    syncOptions:
   459→    - CreateNamespace=true
   460→    retry:
   461→      limit: 5
   462→      backoff:
   463→        duration: 5s
   464→        factor: 2
   465→        maxDuration: 3m
   466→```

   468→## State of the Art
   469→
   470→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   471→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   472→| Apps export directly to visualization tool | OTEL Collector as intermediary | 2022+ (OTEL Collector GA) | Standard practice — decouples apps from backend |
   473→| Aspire Dashboard requires AppHost | Standalone Docker image available | Aspire 8.0+ (2024) | Dashboard usable in any K8s cluster |
   474→| `ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS` | `DOTNET_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS` | Aspire 9.x | Env var name changed — use the `DOTNET_` prefix |
   475→| OTEL Collector `memory_limiter` uses `limit_mib` | `limit_percentage` preferred for containers | 2023+ | Scales with container resource limits automatically |
   476→| Dashboard OTLP on port 4317 | Dashboard OTLP on port 18889 (internal) | Aspire 9.x | Docker maps 4317->18889 but K8s should target 18889 directly |
   477→
   478→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   479→- `ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS` — replaced by `DOTNET_DASHBOARD_UNSECURED_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS` in newer versions (both may work for backwards compatibility)
   480→- OTEL Collector contrib example at `0.51.0` in GitHub — very outdated; current is `0.146.0`
   481→
   482→## Open Questions
   483→
   484→1. **Exact Aspire Dashboard image tag alignment with project SDK**
   485→   - What we know: Project uses Aspire SDK 13.1.0. Dashboard standalone image uses its own version scheme (currently 9.x).
   486→   - What's unclear: Whether `9.3` or `latest` is the best tag for the standalone dashboard image.
   487→   - Recommendation: Use `9.3` for reproducibility. Can switch to `latest` if specific features are needed. The dashboard image tag does NOT need to match the SDK version.
   488→
   489→2. **Kind cluster recreation required**
   490→   - What we know: Adding a new NodePort mapping (30888 for Dashboard) requires updating kind-config.yaml. Kind does not support hot-adding port mappings.
   491→   - What's unclear: Whether the bootstrap script documentation should emphasize cluster recreation.
   492→   - Recommendation: Update kind-config.yaml and document that existing clusters must be recreated. The bootstrap script already handles idempotent cluster creation.
   493→
   494→3. **OTEL Collector core vs contrib image**
   495→   - What we know: Core image has OTLP receiver, batch processor, memory_limiter, OTLP exporter. Contrib adds k8sattributes, Prometheus, etc.
   496→   - What's unclear: Whether the core image includes the health_check extension.
   497→   - Recommendation: Use core image (`otel/opentelemetry-collector:0.146.0`). Health check extension is included in core by default. If issues arise, switch to contrib.
   498→
   499→## Validation Architecture
   500→
   501→### Test Framework
   502→
   503→| Property | Value |
   504→|----------|-------|
   505→| Framework | Manual validation via kubectl + curl (infrastructure-only phase) |
   506→| Config file | N/A — no application code changes |
   507→| Quick run command | `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -l app=otel-collector && kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -l app=aspire-dashboard` |
   508→| Full suite command | `kubectl logs deployment/otel-collector -n micro-commerce --tail=20 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:38888` |
   509→
   510→### Phase Requirements -> Test Map
   511→
   512→| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
   513→|--------|----------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   514→| OBSV-01 | OTEL Collector receives OTLP and forwards to Dashboard | smoke | `kubectl logs deployment/otel-collector -n micro-commerce \| grep -i "exporting"` | N/A — infrastructure validation |
   515→| OBSV-01 | ApiService/Gateway have OTLP endpoint configured | smoke | `kubectl get deployment apiservice -n micro-commerce -o yaml \| grep OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` | N/A — manifest check |
   516→| OBSV-02 | Aspire Dashboard is accessible at localhost:38888 | smoke | `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:38888` (expect 200) | N/A — curl check |
   517→| OBSV-02 | Dashboard shows live traces | manual-only | Open `http://localhost:38888` in browser, trigger checkout flow, verify traces visible | N/A — requires human visual verification |
   518→| Success-3 | OTEL Collector pod stays healthy | smoke | `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -l app=otel-collector -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.containerStatuses[0].restartCount}'` (expect 0) | N/A — kubectl check |
   519→
   520→### Sampling Rate
   521→
   522→- **Per task commit:** `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` (verify pod status)
   523→- **Per wave merge:** Full smoke commands above
   524→- **Phase gate:** All smoke checks pass + manual Dashboard trace verification
   525→
   526→### Wave 0 Gaps
   527→
   528→None — this phase is purely Kubernetes manifest work. No test framework setup needed. Validation is smoke tests via kubectl and curl against a running kind cluster.

    46→<context_fidelity>
    47→## CRITICAL: User Decision Fidelity
    48→
    49→The orchestrator provides user decisions in `<user_decisions>` tags from `/gsd:discuss-phase`.
    50→
    51→**Before creating ANY task, verify:**
    52→
    53→1. **Locked Decisions (from `## Decisions`)** — MUST be implemented exactly as specified
    54→   - If user said "use library X" → task MUST use library X, not an alternative
    55→   - If user said "card layout" → task MUST implement cards, not tables
    56→   - If user said "no animations" → task MUST NOT include animations
    57→
    58→2. **Deferred Ideas (from `## Deferred Ideas`)** — MUST NOT appear in plans
    59→   - If user deferred "search functionality" → NO search tasks allowed
    60→   - If user deferred "dark mode" → NO dark mode tasks allowed
    61→
    62→3. **Claude's Discretion (from `## Claude's Discretion`)** — Use your judgment
    63→   - Make reasonable choices and document in task actions
    64→
    65→**Self-check before returning:** For each plan, verify:
    66→- [ ] Every locked decision has a task implementing it
    67→- [ ] No task implements a deferred idea
    68→- [ ] Discretion areas are handled reasonably
    69→
    70→**If conflict exists** (e.g., research suggests library Y but user locked library X):
    71→- Honor the user's locked decision
    72→- Note in task action: "Using X per user decision (research suggested Y)"
    73→</context_fidelity>

    75→<philosophy>
    76→
    77→## Solo Developer + Claude Workflow
    78→
    79→Planning for ONE person (the user) and ONE implementer (Claude).
    80→- No teams, stakeholders, ceremonies, coordination overhead
    81→- User = visionary/product owner, Claude = builder
    82→- Estimate effort in Claude execution time, not human dev time
    83→
    84→## Plans Are Prompts
    85→
    86→PLAN.md IS the prompt (not a document that becomes one). Contains:
    87→- Objective (what and why)
    88→- Context (@file references)
    89→- Tasks (with verification criteria)
    90→- Success criteria (measurable)
    91→
    92→## Quality Degradation Curve
    93→
    94→| Context Usage | Quality | Claude's State |
    95→|---------------|---------|----------------|
    96→| 0-30% | PEAK | Thorough, comprehensive |
    97→| 30-50% | GOOD | Confident, solid work |
    98→| 50-70% | DEGRADING | Efficiency mode begins |
    99→| 70%+ | POOR | Rushed, minimal |
   100→
   101→**Rule:** Plans should complete within ~50% context. More plans, smaller scope, consistent quality. Each plan: 2-3 tasks max.
   102→
   103→## Ship Fast
   104→
   105→Plan -> Execute -> Ship -> Learn -> Repeat
   106→
   107→**Anti-enterprise patterns (delete if seen):**
   108→- Team structures, RACI matrices, stakeholder management
   109→- Sprint ceremonies, change management processes
   110→- Human dev time estimates (hours, days, weeks)
   111→- Documentation for documentation's sake

   115→<discovery_levels>
   116→
   117→## Mandatory Discovery Protocol
   118→
   119→Discovery is MANDATORY unless you can prove current context exists.
   120→
   121→**Level 0 - Skip** (pure internal work, existing patterns only)
   122→- ALL work follows established codebase patterns (grep confirms)
   123→- No new external dependencies
   124→- Examples: Add delete button, add field to model, create CRUD endpoint
   125→
   126→**Level 1 - Quick Verification** (2-5 min)
   127→- Single known library, confirming syntax/version
   128→- Action: Context7 resolve-library-id + query-docs, no DISCOVERY.md needed
   129→
   130→**Level 2 - Standard Research** (15-30 min)
   131→- Choosing between 2-3 options, new external integration
   132→- Action: Route to discovery workflow, produces DISCOVERY.md
   133→
   134→**Level 3 - Deep Dive** (1+ hour)
   135→- Architectural decision with long-term impact, novel problem
   136→- Action: Full research with DISCOVERY.md
   137→
   138→**Depth indicators:**
   139→- Level 2+: New library not in package.json, external API, "choose/select/evaluate" in description
   140→- Level 3: "architecture/design/system", multiple external services, data modeling, auth design
   141→
   142→For niche domains (3D, games, audio, shaders, ML), suggest `/gsd:research-phase` before plan-phase.
   143→
   144→</discovery_levels>
   145→
   146→<task_breakdown>
   147→
   148→## Task Anatomy
   149→
   150→Every task has four required fields:
   151→
   152→**<files>:** Exact file paths created or modified.
   153→- Good: `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`, `prisma/schema.prisma`
   154→- Bad: "the auth files", "relevant components"
   155→
   156→**<action>:** Specific implementation instructions, including what to avoid and WHY.
   157→- Good: "Create POST endpoint accepting {email, password}, validates using bcrypt against User table, returns JWT in httpOnly cookie with 15-min expiry. Use jose library (not jsonwebtoken - CommonJS issues with Edge runtime)."
   158→- Bad: "Add authentication", "Make login work"
   159→
   160→**<verify>:** How to prove the task is complete.
   161→
   162→```xml
   163→<verify>
   164→  <automated>pytest tests/test_module.py::test_behavior -x</automated>
   165→</verify>
   166→```
   167→
   168→- Good: Specific automated command that runs in < 60 seconds
   169→- Bad: "It works", "Looks good", manual-only verification
   170→- Simple format also accepted: `npm test` passes, `curl -X POST /api/auth/login` returns 200
   171→
   172→**Nyquist Rule:** Every `<verify>` must include an `<automated>` command. If no test exists yet, set `<automated>MISSING — Wave 0 must create {test_file} first</automated>` and create a Wave 0 task that generates the test scaffold.
   173→
   174→**<done>:** Acceptance criteria - measurable state of completion.
   175→- Good: "Valid credentials return 200 + JWT cookie, invalid credentials return 401"
   176→- Bad: "Authentication is complete"
   177→
   178→## Task Types
   179→
   180→| Type | Use For | Autonomy |
   181→|------|---------|----------|
   182→| `auto` | Everything Claude can do independently | Fully autonomous |
   183→| `checkpoint:human-verify` | Visual/functional verification | Pauses for user |
   184→| `checkpoint:decision` | Implementation choices | Pauses for user |
   185→| `checkpoint:human-action` | Truly unavoidable manual steps (rare) | Pauses for user |
   186→
   187→**Automation-first rule:** If Claude CAN do it via CLI/API, Claude MUST do it. Checkpoints verify AFTER automation, not replace it.
   188→
   189→## Task Sizing
   190→
   191→Each task: **15-60 minutes** Claude execution time.
   192→
   193→| Duration | Action |
   194→|----------|--------|
   195→| < 15 min | Too small — combine with related task |
   196→| 15-60 min | Right size |
   197→| > 60 min | Too large — split |
   198→
   199→**Too large signals:** Touches >3-5 files, multiple distinct chunks, action section >1 paragraph.
   200→
   201→**Combine signals:** One task sets up for the next, separate tasks touch same file, neither meaningful alone.
   202→
   203→## Interface-First Task Ordering
   204→
   205→When a plan creates new interfaces consumed by subsequent tasks:
   206→
   207→1. **First task: Define contracts** — Create type files, interfaces, exports
   208→2. **Middle tasks: Implement** — Build against the defined contracts
   209→3. **Last task: Wire** — Connect implementations to consumers
   210→
   211→This prevents the "scavenger hunt" anti-pattern where executors explore the codebase to understand contracts. They receive the contracts in the plan itself.
   212→
   213→## Specificity Examples
   214→
   215→| TOO VAGUE | JUST RIGHT |
   216→|-----------|------------|
   217→| "Add authentication" | "Add JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library, store in httpOnly cookie, 15min access / 7day refresh" |
   218→| "Create the API" | "Create POST /api/projects endpoint accepting {name, description}, validates name length 3-50 chars, returns 201 with project object" |
   219→| "Style the dashboard" | "Add Tailwind classes to Dashboard.tsx: grid layout (3 cols on lg, 1 on mobile), card shadows, hover states on action buttons" |
   220→| "Handle errors" | "Wrap API calls in try/catch, return {error: string} on 4xx/5xx, show toast via sonner on client" |
   221→| "Set up the database" | "Add User and Project models to schema.prisma with UUID ids, email unique constraint, createdAt/updatedAt timestamps, run prisma db push" |
   222→
   223→**Test:** Could a different Claude instance execute without asking clarifying questions? If not, add specificity.
   224→
   225→## TDD Detection
   226→
   227→**Heuristic:** Can you write `expect(fn(input)).toBe(output)` before writing `fn`?
   228→- Yes → Create a dedicated TDD plan (type: tdd)
   229→- No → Standard task in standard plan
   230→
   231→**TDD candidates (dedicated TDD plans):** Business logic with defined I/O, API endpoints with request/response contracts, data transformations, validation rules, algorithms, state machines.
   232→
   233→**Standard tasks:** UI layout/styling, configuration, glue code, one-off scripts, simple CRUD with no business logic.
   234→
   235→**Why TDD gets own plan:** TDD requires RED→GREEN→REFACTOR cycles consuming 40-50% context. Embedding in multi-task plans degrades quality.
   236→
   237→**Task-level TDD** (for code-producing tasks in standard plans): When a task creates or modifies production code, add `tdd="true"` and a `<behavior>` block to make test expectations explicit before implementation:
   238→
   239→```xml
   240→<task type="auto" tdd="true">
   241→  <name>Task: [name]</name>
   242→  <files>src/feature.ts, src/feature.test.ts</files>
   243→  <behavior>
   244→    - Test 1: [expected behavior]
   245→    - Test 2: [edge case]
   246→  </behavior>
   247→  <action>[Implementation after tests pass]</action>
   248→  <verify>
   249→    <automated>npm test -- --filter=feature</automated>
   250→  </verify>
   251→  <done>[Criteria]</done>
   252→</task>
   253→```
   254→
   255→Exceptions where `tdd="true"` is not needed: `type="checkpoint:*"` tasks, configuration-only files, documentation, migration scripts, glue code wiring existing tested components, styling-only changes.
   256→
   257→## User Setup Detection
   258→
   259→For tasks involving external services, identify human-required configuration:
   260→
   261→External service indicators: New SDK (`stripe`, `@sendgrid/mail`, `twilio`, `openai`), webhook handlers, OAuth integration, `process.env.SERVICE_*` patterns.
   262→
   263→For each external service, determine:
   264→1. **Env vars needed** — What secrets from dashboards?
   265→2. **Account setup** — Does user need to create an account?
   266→3. **Dashboard config** — What must be configured in external UI?
   267→
   268→Record in `user_setup` frontmatter. Only include what Claude literally cannot do. Do NOT surface in planning output — execute-plan handles presentation.
   269→
   270→</task_breakdown>
   271→
   272→<dependency_graph>
   273→
   274→## Building the Dependency Graph
   275→
   276→**For each task, record:**
   277→- `needs`: What must exist before this runs
   278→- `creates`: What this produces
   279→- `has_checkpoint`: Requires user interaction?
   280→
   281→**Example with 6 tasks:**
   282→
   283→```
   284→Task A (User model): needs nothing, creates src/models/user.ts
   285→Task B (Product model): needs nothing, creates src/models/product.ts
   286→Task C (User API): needs Task A, creates src/api/users.ts
   287→Task D (Product API): needs Task B, creates src/api/products.ts
   288→Task E (Dashboard): needs Task C + D, creates src/components/Dashboard.tsx
   289→Task F (Verify UI): checkpoint:human-verify, needs Task E
   290→
   291→Graph:
   292→  A --> C --\
   293→              --> E --> F
   294→  B --> D --/
   295→
   296→Wave analysis:
   297→  Wave 1: A, B (independent roots)
   298→  Wave 2: C, D (depend only on Wave 1)
   299→  Wave 3: E (depends on Wave 2)
   300→  Wave 4: F (checkpoint, depends on Wave 3)
   301→```
   302→
   303→## Vertical Slices vs Horizontal Layers
   304→
   305→**Vertical slices (PREFER):**
   306→```
   307→Plan 01: User feature (model + API + UI)
   308→Plan 02: Product feature (model + API + UI)
   309→Plan 03: Order feature (model + API + UI)
   310→```
   311→Result: All three run parallel (Wave 1)
   312→
   313→**Horizontal layers (AVOID):**
   314→```
   315→Plan 01: Create User model, Product model, Order model
   316→Plan 02: Create User API, Product API, Order API
   317→Plan 03: Create User UI, Product UI, Order UI
   318→```
   319→Result: Fully sequential (02 needs 01, 03 needs 02)
   320→
   321→**When vertical slices work:** Features are independent, self-contained, no cross-feature dependencies.
   322→
   323→**When horizontal layers necessary:** Shared foundation required (auth before protected features), genuine type dependencies, infrastructure setup.
   324→
   325→## File Ownership for Parallel Execution
   326→
   327→Exclusive file ownership prevents conflicts:
   328→
   329→```yaml
   330→# Plan 01 frontmatter
   331→files_modified: [src/models/user.ts, src/api/users.ts]
   332→
   333→# Plan 02 frontmatter (no overlap = parallel)
   334→files_modified: [src/models/product.ts, src/api/products.ts]
   335→```
   336→
   337→No overlap → can run parallel. File in multiple plans → later plan depends on earlier.
   338→
   339→</dependency_graph>
   340→
   341→<scope_estimation>
   342→
   343→## Context Budget Rules
   344→
   345→Plans should complete within ~50% context (not 80%). No context anxiety, quality maintained start to finish, room for unexpected complexity.
   346→
   347→**Each plan: 2-3 tasks maximum.**
   348→
   349→| Task Complexity | Tasks/Plan | Context/Task | Total |
   350→|-----------------|------------|--------------|-------|
   351→| Simple (CRUD, config) | 3 | ~10-15% | ~30-45% |
   352→| Complex (auth, payments) | 2 | ~20-30% | ~40-50% |
   353→| Very complex (migrations) | 1-2 | ~30-40% | ~30-50% |
   354→
   355→## Split Signals
   356→
   357→**ALWAYS split if:**
   358→- More than 3 tasks
   359→- Multiple subsystems (DB + API + UI = separate plans)
   360→- Any task with >5 file modifications
   361→- Checkpoint + implementation in same plan
   362→- Discovery + implementation in same plan
   363→
   364→**CONSIDER splitting:** >5 files total, complex domains, uncertainty about approach, natural semantic boundaries.
   365→
   366→## Depth Calibration
   367→
   368→| Depth | Typical Plans/Phase | Tasks/Plan |
   369→|-------|---------------------|------------|
   370→| Quick | 1-3 | 2-3 |
   371→| Standard | 3-5 | 2-3 |
   372→| Comprehensive | 5-10 | 2-3 |
   373→
   374→Derive plans from actual work. Depth determines compression tolerance, not a target. Don't pad small work to hit a number. Don't compress complex work to look efficient.
   375→
   376→## Context Per Task Estimates
   377→
   378→| Files Modified | Context Impact |
   379→|----------------|----------------|
   380→| 0-3 files | ~10-15% (small) |
   381→| 4-6 files | ~20-30% (medium) |
   382→| 7+ files | ~40%+ (split) |
   383→
   384→| Complexity | Context/Task |
   385→|------------|--------------|
   386→| Simple CRUD | ~15% |
   387→| Business logic | ~25% |
   388→| Complex algorithms | ~40% |
   389→| Domain modeling | ~35% |
   390→
   391→</scope_estimation>

   461→## Frontmatter Fields
   462→
   463→| Field | Required | Purpose |
   464→|-------|----------|---------|
   465→| `phase` | Yes | Phase identifier (e.g., `01-foundation`) |
   466→| `plan` | Yes | Plan number within phase |

   471→| `autonomous` | Yes | `true` if no checkpoints |

   474→| `must_haves` | Yes | Goal-backward verification criteria |

   476→Wave numbers are pre-computed during planning. Execute-phase reads `wave` directly from frontmatter.

   568→
   569→## Goal-Backward Methodology
   570→
   571→**Forward planning:** "What should we build?" → produces tasks.
   572→**Goal-backward:** "What must be TRUE for the goal to be achieved?" → produces requirements tasks must satisfy.
   573→
   574→## The Process
   575→
   576→**Step 0: Extract Requirement IDs**
   577→Read ROADMAP.md `**Requirements:**` line for this phase. Strip brackets if present (e.g., `[AUTH-01, AUTH-02]` → `AUTH-01, AUTH-02`). Distribute requirement IDs across plans — each plan's `requirements` frontmatter field MUST list the IDs its tasks address. **CRITICAL:** Every requirement ID MUST appear in at least one plan. Plans with an empty `requirements` field are invalid.
   578→
   579→**Step 1: State the Goal**
   580→Take phase goal from ROADMAP.md. Must be outcome-shaped, not task-shaped.
   581→- Good: "Working chat interface" (outcome)
   582→- Bad: "Build chat components" (task)
   583→
   584→**Step 2: Derive Observable Truths**
   585→"What must be TRUE for this goal to be achieved?" List 3-7 truths from USER's perspective.
   586→
   587→For "working chat interface":
   588→- User can see existing messages
   589→- User can type a new message
   590→- User can send the message
   591→- Sent message appears in the list
   592→- Messages persist across page refresh
   593→
   594→**Test:** Each truth verifiable by a human using the application.
   595→
   596→**Step 3: Derive Required Artifacts**
   597→For each truth: "What must EXIST for this to be true?"
   598→
   599→"User can see existing messages" requires:
   600→- Message list component (renders Message[])
   601→- Messages state (loaded from somewhere)
   602→- API route or data source (provides messages)
   603→- Message type definition (shapes the data)
   604→
   605→**Test:** Each artifact = a specific file or database object.
   606→
   607→**Step 4: Derive Required Wiring**
   608→For each artifact: "What must be CONNECTED for this to function?"
   609→
   610→Message list component wiring:
   611→- Imports Message type (not using `any`)
   612→- Receives messages prop or fetches from API
   613→- Maps over messages to render (not hardcoded)
   614→- Handles empty state (not just crashes)
   615→
   616→**Step 5: Identify Key Links**
   617→"Where is this most likely to break?" Key links = critical connections where breakage causes cascading failures.
   618→
   619→For chat interface:
   620→- Input onSubmit -> API call (if broken: typing works but sending doesn't)
   621→- API save -> database (if broken: appears to send but doesn't persist)
   622→- Component -> real data (if broken: shows placeholder, not messages)
   623→
   624→## Must-Haves Output Format
   625→
   626→```yaml
   627→must_haves:
   628→  truths:
   629→    - "User can see existing messages"
   630→    - "User can send a message"
   631→    - "Messages persist across refresh"
   632→  artifacts:
   633→    - path: "src/components/Chat.tsx"
   634→      provides: "Message list rendering"
   635→      min_lines: 30
   636→    - path: "src/app/api/chat/route.ts"
   637→      provides: "Message CRUD operations"
   638→      exports: ["GET", "POST"]
   639→    - path: "prisma/schema.prisma"
   640→      provides: "Message model"
   641→      contains: "model Message"
   642→  key_links:
   643→    - from: "src/components/Chat.tsx"
   644→      to: "/api/chat"
   645→      via: "fetch in useEffect"
   646→      pattern: "fetch.*api/chat"
   647→    - from: "src/app/api/chat/route.ts"
   648→      to: "prisma.message"
   649→      via: "database query"
   650→      pattern: "prisma\\.message\\.(find|create)"
   651→```
   652→
   653→## Common Failures
   654→
   655→**Truths too vague:**
   656→- Bad: "User can use chat"
   657→- Good: "User can see messages", "User can send message", "Messages persist"
   658→
   659→**Artifacts too abstract:**
   660→- Bad: "Chat system", "Auth module"
   661→- Good: "src/components/Chat.tsx", "src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts"
   662→
   663→**Missing wiring:**
   664→- Bad: Listing components without how they connect
   665→- Good: "Chat.tsx fetches from /api/chat via useEffect on mount"
   666→
   667→</goal_backward>
   668→
   669→<checkpoints>
   670→
   671→## Checkpoint Types
   672→
   673→**checkpoint:human-verify (90% of checkpoints)**
   674→Human confirms Claude's automated work works correctly.
   675→
   676→Use for: Visual UI checks, interactive flows, functional verification, animation/accessibility.
   677→
   678→```xml
   679→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify" gate="blocking">
   680→  <what-built>[What Claude automated]</what-built>
   681→  <how-to-verify>
   682→    [Exact steps to test - URLs, commands, expected behavior]
   683→  </how-to-verify>
   684→  <resume-signal>Type "approved" or describe issues</resume-signal>
   685→</task>
   686→```
   687→
   688→**checkpoint:decision (9% of checkpoints)**
   689→Human makes implementation choice affecting direction.
   690→
   691→Use for: Technology selection, architecture decisions, design choices.
   692→
   693→```xml
   694→<task type="checkpoint:decision" gate="blocking">
   695→  <decision>[What's being decided]</decision>
   696→  <context>[Why this matters]</context>
   697→  <options>
   698→    <option id="option-a">
   699→      <name>[Name]</name>
   700→      <pros>[Benefits]</pros>
   701→      <cons>[Tradeoffs]</cons>
   702→    </option>
   703→  </options>
   704→  <resume-signal>Select: option-a, option-b, or ...</resume-signal>
   705→</task>
   706→```
   707→
   708→**checkpoint:human-action (1% - rare)**
   709→Action has NO CLI/API and requires human-only interaction.
   710→
   711→Use ONLY for: Email verification links, SMS 2FA codes, manual account approvals, credit card 3D Secure flows.
   712→
   713→Do NOT use for: Deploying (use CLI), creating webhooks (use API), creating databases (use provider CLI), running builds/tests (use Bash), creating files (use Write).
   714→
   715→## Authentication Gates
   716→
   717→When Claude tries CLI/API and gets auth error → creates checkpoint → user authenticates → Claude retries. Auth gates are created dynamically, NOT pre-planned.
   718→
   719→## Writing Guidelines
   720→
   721→**DO:** Automate everything before checkpoint, be specific ("Visit https://myapp.vercel.app" not "check deployment"), number verification steps, state expected outcomes.
   722→
   723→**DON'T:** Ask human to do work Claude can automate, mix multiple verifications, place checkpoints before automation completes.
   724→
   725→## Anti-Patterns
   726→
   727→**Bad - Asking human to automate:**
   728→```xml
   729→<task type="checkpoint:human-action">
   730→  <action>Deploy to Vercel</action>
   731→  <instructions>Visit vercel.com, import repo, click deploy...</instructions>
   732→</task>
   733→```
   734→Why bad: Vercel has a CLI. Claude should run `vercel --yes`.
   735→
   736→**Bad - Too many checkpoints:**
   737→```xml
   738→<task type="auto">Create schema</task>
   739→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify">Check schema</task>
   740→<task type="auto">Create API</task>
   741→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify">Check API</task>
   742→```
   743→Why bad: Verification fatigue. Combine into one checkpoint at end.
   744→
   745→**Good - Single verification checkpoint:**
   746→```xml
   747→<task type="auto">Create schema</task>
   748→<task type="auto">Create API</task>
   749→<task type="auto">Create UI</task>
   750→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify">
   751→  <what-built>Complete auth flow (schema + API + UI)</what-built>
   752→  <how-to-verify>Test full flow: register, login, access protected page</how-to-verify>
   753→</task>
   754→```
   755→
   756→</checkpoints>
   757→
   758→<tdd_integration>
   759→
   760→## TDD Plan Structure
   761→
   762→TDD candidates identified in task_breakdown get dedicated plans (type: tdd). One feature per TDD plan.
   763→
   764→```markdown
   765→---
   766→phase: XX-name
   767→plan: NN
   768→type: tdd
   769→---
   770→
   771→<objective>
   772→[What feature and why]
   773→Purpose: [Design benefit of TDD for this feature]
   774→Output: [Working, tested feature]
   775→</objective>
   776→
   777→<feature>
   778→  <name>[Feature name]</name>
   779→  <files>[source file, test file]</files>
   780→  <behavior>
   781→    [Expected behavior in testable terms]
   782→    Cases: input -> expected output
   783→  </behavior>
   784→  <implementation>[How to implement once tests pass]</implementation>
   785→</feature>
   786→```
   787→
   788→## Red-Green-Refactor Cycle
   789→
   790→**RED:** Create test file → write test describing expected behavior → run test (MUST fail) → commit: `test({phase}-{plan}): add failing test for [feature]`
   791→
   792→**GREEN:** Write minimal code to pass → run test (MUST pass) → commit: `feat({phase}-{plan}): implement [feature]`
   793→
   794→**REFACTOR (if needed):** Clean up → run tests (MUST pass) → commit: `refactor({phase}-{plan}): clean up [feature]`
   795→
   796→Each TDD plan produces 2-3 atomic commits.
   797→
   798→## Context Budget for TDD
   799→
   800→TDD plans target ~40% context (lower than standard 50%). The RED→GREEN→REFACTOR back-and-forth with file reads, test runs, and output analysis is heavier than linear execution.
   801→
   802→</tdd_integration>
   803→
   804→<gap_closure_mode>
   805→
   806→## Planning from Verification Gaps
   807→
   808→Triggered by `--gaps` flag. Creates plans to address verification or UAT failures.
   809→
   810→**1. Find gap sources:**
   811→
   812→Use init context (from load_project_state) which provides `phase_dir`:
   813→
   814→```bash
   815→# Check for VERIFICATION.md (code verification gaps)
   816→ls "$phase_dir"/*-VERIFICATION.md 2>/dev/null
   817→
   818→# Check for UAT.md with diagnosed status (user testing gaps)
   819→grep -l "status: diagnosed" "$phase_dir"/*-UAT.md 2>/dev/null
   820→```
   821→
   822→**2. Parse gaps:** Each gap has: truth (failed behavior), reason, artifacts (files with issues), missing (things to add/fix).
   823→
   824→**3. Load existing SUMMARYs** to understand what's already built.
   825→
   826→**4. Find next plan number:** If plans 01-03 exist, next is 04.
   827→
   828→**5. Group gaps into plans** by: same artifact, same concern, dependency order (can't wire if artifact is stub → fix stub first).
   829→
   830→**6. Create gap closure tasks:**
   831→
   832→```xml
   833→<task name="{fix_description}" type="auto">
   834→  <files>{artifact.path}</files>
   835→  <action>
   836→    {For each item in gap.missing:}
   837→    - {missing item}
   838→
   839→    Reference existing code: {from SUMMARYs}
   840→    Gap reason: {gap.reason}
   841→  </action>
   842→  <verify>{How to confirm gap is closed}</verify>
   843→  <done>{Observable truth now achievable}</done>
   844→</task>
   845→```
   846→
   847→**7. Write PLAN.md files:**
   848→
   849→```yaml
   850→---
   851→phase: XX-name
   852→plan: NN              # Sequential after existing
   853→type: execute
   854→wave: 1               # Gap closures typically single wave
   855→depends_on: []
   856→files_modified: [...]
   857→autonomous: true
   858→gap_closure: true     # Flag for tracking
   859→---
   860→```
   861→
   862→</gap_closure_mode>
   863→
   864→<revision_mode>
   865→
   866→## Planning from Checker Feedback
   867→
   868→Triggered when orchestrator provides `<revision_context>` with checker issues. NOT starting fresh — making targeted updates to existing plans.
   869→
   870→**Mindset:** Surgeon, not architect. Minimal changes for specific issues.
   871→
   872→### Step 1: Load Existing Plans
   873→
   874→```bash
   875→cat .planning/phases/$PHASE-*/$PHASE-*-PLAN.md
   876→```
   877→
   878→Build mental model of current plan structure, existing tasks, must_haves.
   879→
   880→### Step 2: Parse Checker Issues
   881→
   882→Issues come in structured format:
   883→
   884→```yaml
   885→issues:
   886→  - plan: "16-01"
   887→    dimension: "task_completeness"
   888→    severity: "blocker"
   889→    description: "Task 2 missing <verify> element"
   890→    fix_hint: "Add verification command for build output"
   891→```
   892→
   893→Group by plan, dimension, severity.
   894→
   895→### Step 3: Revision Strategy
   896→
   897→| Dimension | Strategy |
   898→|-----------|----------|
   899→| requirement_coverage | Add task(s) for missing requirement |
   900→| task_completeness | Add missing elements to existing task |
   901→| dependency_correctness | Fix depends_on, recompute waves |
   902→| key_links_planned | Add wiring task or update action |
   903→| scope_sanity | Split into multiple plans |
   904→| must_haves_derivation | Derive and add must_haves to frontmatter |
   905→
   906→### Step 4: Make Targeted Updates
   907→
   908→**DO:** Edit specific flagged sections, preserve working parts, update waves if dependencies change.
   909→
   910→**DO NOT:** Rewrite entire plans for minor issues, add unnecessary tasks, break existing working plans.
   911→
   912→### Step 5: Validate Changes
   913→
   914→- [ ] All flagged issues addressed
   915→- [ ] No new issues introduced
   916→- [ ] Wave numbers still valid
   917→- [ ] Dependencies still correct
   918→- [ ] Files on disk updated
   919→
   920→### Step 6: Commit
   921→
   922→```bash
   923→node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" commit "fix($PHASE): revise plans based on checker feedback" --files .planning/phases/$PHASE-*/$PHASE-*-PLAN.md
   924→```
   925→
   926→### Step 7: Return Revision Summary
   927→
   928→```markdown
   929→## REVISION COMPLETE
   930→
   931→**Issues addressed:** {N}/{M}
   932→
   933→### Changes Made
   934→
   935→| Plan | Change | Issue Addressed |
   936→|------|--------|-----------------|
   937→| 16-01 | Added <verify> to Task 2 | task_completeness |
   938→| 16-02 | Added logout task | requirement_coverage (AUTH-02) |
   939→
   940→### Files Updated
   941→
   942→- .planning/phases/16-xxx/16-01-PLAN.md
   943→- .planning/phases/16-xxx/16-02-PLAN.md
   944→
   945→{If any issues NOT addressed:}
   946→
   947→### Unaddressed Issues
   948→
   949→| Issue | Reason |
   950→|-------|--------|
   951→| {issue} | {why - needs user input, architectural change, etc.} |
   952→```
   953→
   954→</revision_mode>
   955→
   956→<execution_flow>
   957→
   958→<step name="load_project_state" priority="first">
   959→Load planning context:
   960→
   961→```bash
   962→INIT=$(node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" init plan-phase "${PHASE}")
   963→```
   964→
   965→Extract from init JSON: `planner_model`, `researcher_model`, `checker_model`, `commit_docs`, `research_enabled`, `phase_dir`, `phase_number`, `has_research`, `has_context`.
   966→
   967→Also read STATE.md for position, decisions, blockers:
   968→```bash
   969→cat .planning/STATE.md 2>/dev/null
   970→```
   971→
   972→If STATE.md missing but .planning/ exists, offer to reconstruct or continue without.
   973→</step>
   974→
   975→<step name="load_codebase_context">
   976→Check for codebase map:
   977→
   978→```bash
   979→ls .planning/codebase/*.md 2>/dev/null
   980→```
   981→
   982→If exists, load relevant documents by phase type:
   983→
   984→| Phase Keywords | Load These |
   985→|----------------|------------|
   986→| UI, frontend, components | CONVENTIONS.md, STRUCTURE.md |
   987→| API, backend, endpoints | ARCHITECTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md |
   988→| database, schema, models | ARCHITECTURE.md, STACK.md |
   989→| testing, tests | TESTING.md, CONVENTIONS.md |
   990→| integration, external API | INTEGRATIONS.md, STACK.md |
   991→| refactor, cleanup | CONCERNS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md |
   992→| setup, config | STACK.md, STRUCTURE.md |
   993→| (default) | STACK.md, ARCHITECTURE.md |
   994→</step>
   995→
   996→<step name="identify_phase">
   997→```bash
   998→cat .planning/ROADMAP.md
   999→ls .planning/phases/
  1000→```
  1001→
  1002→If multiple phases available, ask which to plan. If obvious (first incomplete), proceed.
  1003→
  1004→Read existing PLAN.md or DISCOVERY.md in phase directory.
  1005→
  1006→**If `--gaps` flag:** Switch to gap_closure_mode.
  1007→</step>
  1008→
  1009→<step name="mandatory_discovery">
  1010→Apply discovery level protocol (see discovery_levels section).
  1011→</step>
  1012→
  1013→<step name="read_project_history">
  1014→**Two-step context assembly: digest for selection, full read for understanding.**
  1015→
  1016→**Step 1 — Generate digest index:**
  1017→```bash
  1018→node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" history-digest
  1019→```
  1020→
  1021→**Step 2 — Select relevant phases (typically 2-4):**
  1022→
  1023→Score each phase by relevance to current work:
  1024→- `affects` overlap: Does it touch same subsystems?
  1025→- `provides` dependency: Does current phase need what it created?
  1026→- `patterns`: Are its patterns applicable?
  1027→- Roadmap: Marked as explicit dependency?
  1028→
  1029→Select top 2-4 phases. Skip phases with no relevance signal.
  1030→
  1031→**Step 3 — Read full SUMMARYs for selected phases:**
  1032→```bash
  1033→cat .planning/phases/{selected-phase}/*-SUMMARY.md
  1034→```
  1035→
  1036→From full SUMMARYs extract:
  1037→- How things were implemented (file patterns, code structure)
  1038→- Why decisions were made (context, tradeoffs)
  1039→- What problems were solved (avoid repeating)
  1040→- Actual artifacts created (realistic expectations)
  1041→
  1042→**Step 4 — Keep digest-level context for unselected phases:**
  1043→
  1044→For phases not selected, retain from digest:
  1045→- `tech_stack`: Available libraries
  1046→- `decisions`: Constraints on approach
  1047→- `patterns`: Conventions to follow
  1048→
  1049→**From STATE.md:** Decisions → constrain approach. Pending todos → candidates.
  1050→
  1051→**From RETROSPECTIVE.md (if exists):**
  1052→```bash
  1053→cat .planning/RETROSPECTIVE.md 2>/dev/null | tail -100
  1054→```
  1055→
  1056→Read the most recent milestone retrospective and cross-milestone trends. Extract:
  1057→- **Patterns to follow** from "What Worked" and "Patterns Established"
  1058→- **Patterns to avoid** from "What Was Inefficient" and "Key Lessons"
  1059→- **Cost patterns** to inform model selection and agent strategy
  1060→</step>
  1061→
  1062→<step name="gather_phase_context">
  1063→Use `phase_dir` from init context (already loaded in load_project_state).
  1064→
  1065→```bash
  1066→cat "$phase_dir"/*-CONTEXT.md 2>/dev/null   # From /gsd:discuss-phase
  1067→cat "$phase_dir"/*-RESEARCH.md 2>/dev/null   # From /gsd:research-phase
  1068→cat "$phase_dir"/*-DISCOVERY.md 2>/dev/null  # From mandatory discovery
  1069→```
  1070→
  1071→**If CONTEXT.md exists (has_context=true from init):** Honor user's vision, prioritize essential features, respect boundaries. Locked decisions — do not revisit.
  1072→
  1073→**If RESEARCH.md exists (has_research=true from init):** Use standard_stack, architecture_patterns, dont_hand_roll, common_pitfalls.
  1074→</step>
  1075→
  1076→<step name="break_into_tasks">
  1077→Decompose phase into tasks. **Think dependencies first, not sequence.**
  1078→
  1079→For each task:
  1080→1. What does it NEED? (files, types, APIs that must exist)
  1081→2. What does it CREATE? (files, types, APIs others might need)
  1082→3. Can it run independently? (no dependencies = Wave 1 candidate)
  1083→
  1084→Apply TDD detection heuristic. Apply user setup detection.
  1085→</step>
  1086→
  1087→<step name="build_dependency_graph">
  1088→Map dependencies explicitly before grouping into plans. Record needs/creates/has_checkpoint for each task.
  1089→
  1090→Identify parallelization: No deps = Wave 1, depends only on Wave 1 = Wave 2, shared file conflict = sequential.
  1091→
  1092→Prefer vertical slices over horizontal layers.
  1093→</step>
  1094→
  1095→<step name="assign_waves">
  1096→```
  1097→waves = {}
  1098→for each plan in plan_order:
  1099→  if plan.depends_on is empty:
  1100→    plan.wave = 1
  1101→  else:
  1102→    plan.wave = max(waves[dep] for dep in plan.depends_on) + 1
  1103→  waves[plan.id] = plan.wave
  1104→```
  1105→</step>
  1106→
  1107→<step name="group_into_plans">
  1108→Rules:
  1109→1. Same-wave tasks with no file conflicts → parallel plans
  1110→2. Shared files → same plan or sequential plans
  1111→3. Checkpoint tasks → `autonomous: false`
  1112→4. Each plan: 2-3 tasks, single concern, ~50% context target
  1113→</step>
  1114→
  1115→<step name="derive_must_haves">
  1116→Apply goal-backward methodology (see goal_backward section):
  1117→1. State the goal (outcome, not task)
  1118→2. Derive observable truths (3-7, user perspective)
  1119→3. Derive required artifacts (specific files)
  1120→4. Derive required wiring (connections)
  1121→5. Identify key links (critical connections)
  1122→</step>
  1123→
  1124→<step name="estimate_scope">
  1125→Verify each plan fits context budget: 2-3 tasks, ~50% target. Split if necessary. Check depth setting.
  1126→</step>
  1127→
  1128→<step name="confirm_breakdown">
  1129→Present breakdown with wave structure. Wait for confirmation in interactive mode. Auto-approve in yolo mode.
  1130→</step>
  1131→
  1132→<step name="write_phase_prompt">
  1133→Use template structure for each PLAN.md.
  1134→
  1135→**ALWAYS use the Write tool to create files** — never use `Bash(cat << 'EOF')` or heredoc commands for file creation.
  1136→
  1137→Write to `.planning/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{NN}-PLAN.md`
  1138→
  1139→Include all frontmatter fields.
  1140→</step>
  1141→
  1142→<step name="validate_plan">
  1143→Validate each created PLAN.md using gsd-tools:
  1144→
  1145→```bash
  1146→VALID=$(node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" frontmatter validate "$PLAN_PATH" --schema plan)
  1147→```
  1148→
  1149→Returns JSON: `{ valid, missing, present, schema }`
  1150→
  1151→**If `valid=false`:** Fix missing required fields before proceeding.
  1152→
  1153→Required plan frontmatter fields:
  1154→- `phase`, `plan`, `type`, `wave`, `depends_on`, `files_modified`, `autonomous`, `must_haves`
  1155→
  1156→Also validate plan structure:
  1157→
  1158→```bash
  1159→STRUCTURE=$(node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" verify plan-structure "$PLAN_PATH")
  1160→```
  1161→
  1162→Returns JSON: `{ valid, errors, warnings, task_count, tasks }`
  1163→
  1164→**If errors exist:** Fix before committing:
  1165→- Missing `<name>` in task → add name element
  1166→- Missing `<action>` → add action element
  1167→- Checkpoint/autonomous mismatch → update `autonomous: false`
  1168→</step>
  1169→

  1171→Update ROADMAP.md to finalize phase placeholders:
  1172→
  1173→1. Read `.planning/ROADMAP.md`
  1174→2. Find phase entry (`### Phase {N}:`)
  1175→3. Update placeholders:
  1176→
  1177→**Goal** (only if placeholder):
  1178→- `[To be planned]` → derive from CONTEXT.md > RESEARCH.md > phase description
  1179→- If Goal already has real content → leave it
  1180→
  1181→**Plans** (always update):
  1182→- Update count: `**Plans:** {N} plans`
  1183→
  1184→**Plan list** (always update):
  1185→```
  1186→Plans:
  1187→- [ ] {phase}-01-PLAN.md — {brief objective}
  1188→- [ ] {phase}-02-PLAN.md — {brief objective}
  1189→```
  1190→
  1191→4. Write updated ROADMAP.md
  1192→</step>

  1195→```bash
  1196→node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" commit "docs($PHASE): create phase plan" --files .planning/phases/$PHASE-*/$PHASE-*-PLAN.md .planning/ROADMAP.md
  1197→```
  1198→</step>

  1200→<step name="offer_next">

  1202→</step>

  1208→## Planning Complete
  1209→
  1210→```markdown
  1211→## PLANNING COMPLETE
  1212→
  1213→**Phase:** {phase-name}
  1214→**Plans:** {N} plan(s) in {M} wave(s)
  1215→
  1216→### Wave Structure
  1217→
  1218→| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
  1219→|------|-------|------------|
  1220→| 1 | {plan-01}, {plan-02} | yes, yes |
  1221→| 2 | {plan-03} | no (has checkpoint) |
  1222→
  1223→### Plans Created
  1224→
  1225→| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files |
  1226→|------|-----------|-------|-------|
  1227→| {phase}-01 | [brief] | 2 | [files] |
  1228→| {phase}-02 | [brief] | 3 | [files] |
  1229→
  1230→### Next Steps
  1231→
  1232→Execute: `/gsd:execute-phase {phase}`
  1233→
  1234→<sub>`/clear` first - fresh context window</sub>
  1235→```
  1236→
  1237→## Gap Closure Plans Created
  1238→
  1239→```markdown
  1240→## GAP CLOSURE PLANS CREATED
  1241→
  1242→**Phase:** {phase-name}
  1243→**Closing:** {N} gaps from {VERIFICATION|UAT}.md
  1244→
  1245→### Plans

  1250→
  1251→### Next Steps
  1252→
  1253→Execute: `/gsd:execute-phase {phase} --gaps-only`
  1254→```
  1255→
  1256→## Checkpoint Reached / Revision Complete
  1257→
  1258→Follow templates in checkpoints and revision_mode sections respectively.
  1259→
  1260→</structured_returns>

content.text:
## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 28-observability
**Plans:** 2 plan(s) in 2 wave(s)

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 28-01 | yes |
| 2 | 28-02 | yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|
| 28-01 | Create OTEL Collector and Aspire Dashboard Kubernetes base manifests | 2 | 8 (7 new + 1 modified) |
| 28-02 | Wire OTEL to app services, ArgoCD Applications, kind port mapping | 2 | 5 (2 new + 3 modified) |

### Requirement Coverage

| Requirement | Plan(s) |
|-------------|---------|
| OBSV-01 | 28-01, 28-02 |
| OBSV-02 | 28-01, 28-02 |

### Key Design Decisions

- **No overlay directories for observability services** -- they use fixed public images (not built by CI), so base manifests are sufficient. ArgoCD apps point directly to `base/` paths (same pattern as postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak).
- **No application code changes** -- ServiceDefaults already conditionally enables OTLP export when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set. Only K8s manifest work.
- **OTEL Collector core image** (not contrib) -- only OTLP receiver/exporter, batch, memory_limiter needed. No k8sattributes (avoids RBAC complexity).
- **Kind cluster recreation required** -- new port mapping (30888 -> 38888) for Dashboard UI cannot be hot-added to existing kind clusters.

### Next Steps

Execute: `/gsd:execute-phase 28`

<sub>`/clear` first - fresh context window</sub>

    57→
    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
    65→- [x] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git (completed 2026-03-02)
    66→- [x] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop (completed 2026-03-02)
    67→- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring
    68→
    69→## Phase Details
    70→
    71→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    72→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    73→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    74→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    75→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    76→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    77→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    78→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    79→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    80→**Plans**: TBD
    81→
    82→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    83→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    84→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    85→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    86→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    87→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    88→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    89→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    90→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    91→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    92→**Plans**: 4 plans
    93→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    94→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    95→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    96→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5
    97→
    98→### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
    99→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 24
   101→**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
   104→  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
   105→  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
   106→  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
   107→  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
   108→  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
   109→**Plans**: 3 plans
   110→- [x] 25-01-PLAN.md -- MassTransit RabbitMQ transport switching, health endpoint fix, conditional Azure SDK
   111→- [x] 25-02-PLAN.md -- ApiService, Gateway, Web K8s Deployment/Service manifests, Kustomize base/overlay
   112→- [x] 25-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script extension with app image build, kind load, and full stack deployment
   113→
   114→### Phase 25.1: Update UI based on pencil design with shadcn (INSERTED)
   115→
   116→**Goal:** Refresh all storefront and admin UI screens to match the split per-page pencil design files using existing shadcn/ui primitives and Tailwind CSS. Visual update only -- no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes.
   117→**Requirements**: UI-THEME, UI-HOME, UI-CATALOG, UI-PRODUCT-DETAIL, UI-CART, UI-CHECKOUT, UI-ORDER-CONFIRM, UI-ORDER-HISTORY, UI-PROFILE, UI-AUTH-LOGIN, UI-ADMIN
   118→**Depends on:** Phase 25

   134→
   135→### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
   136→**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
   137→**Depends on**: Phase 25
   138→**Requirements**: GOPS-01, GOPS-02
   139→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   140→  1. ArgoCD UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows all services as Synced and Healthy
   141→  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
   142→  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
   143→**Plans**: 2 plans
   144→- [ ] 26-01-PLAN.md -- ArgoCD manifests, child Applications, Kustomize per-service restructure
   145→- [ ] 26-02-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script ArgoCD integration and GitOps handoff
   146→
   147→### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
   148→**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
   149→**Depends on**: Phase 26
   150→**Requirements**: CICD-02
   151→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   152→  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
   153→  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
   154→**Plans**: TBD
   155→
   156→### Phase 28: Observability
   157→**Goal**: Traces, metrics, and logs from all cluster services are visible in the Aspire Dashboard without requiring .NET Aspire AppHost
   158→**Depends on**: Phase 27
   159→**Requirements**: OBSV-01, OBSV-02

   162→  2. A completed checkout flow produces a distributed trace visible end-to-end in the Dashboard spanning ApiService, the saga, and RabbitMQ consumers
   163→  3. OTEL Collector pod stays healthy under normal load (no OOM kills, no dropped telemetry in logs)
   164→**Plans**: 2 plans
